old_claim.note: API Economy provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct API-economy dependency remains because mid-1990s online payment processing predates the documented web-API ecosystem/API-economy anchor and is better modeled from e-commerce and digital identity infrastructure.

source_shape.source_locator: Director biography section: CyberSource was founded in 1994, built payment-processing capability for software.net, and created a focused payment-processing business in 1996.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The SEC-hosted CyberSource proxy anchors online payment processing to the mid-1990s, before the API-economy node's web-API ecosystem date.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Because the source supports payment-gateway chronology without requiring an API-economy ecosystem, the API edge would make a later platform concept a prerequisite for an earlier payment-processing business.

ontology_before: Digital Payment Gateways depended directly on API Economy, implying the payment-gateway node required a later web-API business ecosystem.
ontology_after: Digital Payment Gateways remains anchored to e-commerce platforms and digital identity/security infrastructure; API Economy remains a later platform-business ecosystem node.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: api_economy is no longer a direct prerequisite for digital_payment_gateways.
would_reject_if: The payment-gateway node were narrowed to modern API-first payment platforms rather than mid-1990s online payment processing.
A stronger source showed that CyberSource-style payment processing depended on an already mature API economy in 1996.
The API Economy node were rescoped back to generic software APIs rather than web-API business ecosystems.
short_reason: Payment gateways predate the API-economy ecosystem anchor.
